entry.title: Re: Automating Provisioning Jobs and Job Logs using SAP Identity Provisioning (IPS) API
entry.content:
Trying to automate daily health check report for your SAP Identity Provisioning (IPS) jobs.

The report should:

  • Be generated for a specific date (e.g. today or a user-specified date)

  • Include all jobs (regardless of status) that ran for a list of known system names

  • Extract and display:

    •  

    • name

    • Job status

    • Start & End times

    • Trigger type

    • Job type

    • Duration

    • Error messages (if any)

  • Be presented in an HTML file with a clean, readable layout

Limitation Description

Missing API supportThe critical endpoints that would let you dynamically query job activity are not available in your SAP IPS tenant
 /jobHistoriesReturns 404 — would’ve allowed fetching job runs per day
/jobExecutionsReturns 404 — would’ve allowed filtering by date/system name
 /jobDefinitionsReturns 404 — would’ve allowed mapping system names to job definitions
Only /jobLogs/{jobId} worksBut it requires you to manually provide job IDs, which change daily
No endpoint to discover jobId by system/dateWhich prevents fully automated discovery of job runs

entry.title: Colgate's Journey with SAP BTP: Preserving HR Admin Functionality from SAP HCM to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Webinar Event Date and Time:   17 July 2025 -   Time: 11:00 EST / 17:00 CET

The Challenge:
When transitioning from on-premises SAP HCM to SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, HRIT leaders face a crucial question: How do you preserve the custom-built functionality your business relies on while embracing cloud benefits?

The Solution:
Please Join SAP customer, Colgate, in this SAP partner-led event with Comentec, as they share their practical journey of leveraging SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to not only maintain but also enhance critical HR administrative capabilities during their cloud migration, resulting in reduced implementation time and improved user satisfaction.

In This Webinar, You'll Discover:

  • Practical Migration Strategies: How Colgate identified, prioritized, and successfully transitioned their essential custom HR functionality to the cloud
  • Skills Transition Framework: How Colgate's ABAP developers adapted their existing skills to cloud development, accelerating adoption while reducing training costs
  • Design Principles: How implementing simple, reusable, and sustainable components created a future-proof HR landscape that's easier to maintain
  • Real-World Examples: See how Colgate preserved vital admin capabilities including:
    • Cloud-based payroll interface management (equivalent to PU12 in SAP HCM)
    • Organizational structure-based selection tools for custom processes

Key Takeaways:

  • Skill Reapplication: Discover how your on-premise ABAP resources can effectively transition to cloud development using SAP BTP
  • Enhanced User Experience: Learn how SAP BTP's Fiori Elements framework offers more robust solutions than your current on-premises environment
  • Strategic Advantage: Understand how designing with SAP standards while building reusable components reduces development effort, accelerates implementation, and simplifies support

Who Should Attend:
● HRIT leaders owning the strategy for SAP SuccessFactors migration
● HR Technology teams concerned about preserving critical custom functionality
● SAP HCM developers looking to transfer their skills to the cloud environment
● Business stakeholders wanting to ensure seamless HR operations during migration

This session builds upon our previous webinars with PepsiCo and on EC Integration, taking your SAP BTP knowledge to the next level with practical implementation insights.

Register now to learn how SAP BTP can turn your cloud migration into an opportunity to create an even better HR technology experience.

entry.title: Error "???COMMON_Survey_Invitation_Content???"
entry.content:
For the past week, several users notified me of the the following error displaying when they try to apply esignature after completing a course. Several courses have been experiencing this error "???COMMON_Survey_Invitation_Content???" 

We did not attach a survey to this item, why would this error display?

Please help me determine the cause of this sudden popup error. Thank you! Connie

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation: Can an automation be ran without the SAP Desktop Agent?
entry.content:
Hello,

I am currently working an SAP Build Process Automation pilot project and have reached a point where the team is ready to start looking at deployment options in terms of how the automation will be ran. It seems like the desktop agent is necessary to run a scheduled unattended trigger. Ultimately, we want to avoid having to setup a virtual machine to carry out this particular automation. The below image contains our automation process and the options we see available. What would be the best way to run this particular scenario without the Desktop Agent? The automation currently uses the Outlook SDK, but research has led us to find that if we use Microsoft 365 SDK instead, we can run this scenario without the agent. Are these findings valid? If not, I would appreciate feedback on the best approach to help us move forward!

 

 

Regards,

Patrick Pienkowski

entry.title: Analisys for Office. Error when trying to create advanced calculation
entry.content:
We have AFO version 2.8.2201.1441 installed. When selecting the "Add Advanced Calculation" option and specifying any type of calculation (e.g., ratio1 + ratio2, ratio1 * 100, etc.), and then pressing OK, the system returns the following errors:

  • Program error in class SAPMSSY1, method UNCAUGHT_EXCEPTION

  • System error in program CL_RSR_OLAP and FORM IF_RSR_OLAP GET_LOCAL_FORMULAS-01

In AFO version 2.7, this functionality operated normally.

We would appreciate your help in identifying the cause and possible solution for this issue.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Stock Room Management
entry.content: @Julien_M , Item category determination occurs in the delivery and is independent of whether you use EWM, Stock Room Management, or even no warehouse solution.  You do not need EWM for it.  There is a separate item *type* determination in the EWM ODO which drives some of the EWM processes *if* you are using EWM.  You would, of course, need EWM for that and not Stock Room Management.

entry.title: Control of special characters/text in SAP PI Mail sender adapter while reading email from Inbox
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I'm using mail sender adapter to read email from inbox, and I set mail attribute to enable Mail package
Encoding format either I chose 'none' or  'base64'

however PI receives special characters inside  of XML after read the email
Example of wrong data format in 'from'  field or  'To'  field or 'Message_ID' field ( where we can special  characters <xxxx>) 
Due to this including special characters format, mapping transformation throws an error.

Is their any special module configuration in Mail sender which avoid special characters while reading the email from Inbox

Kindly please suggest your valuable inputs.

Thank you

Sateesh

entry.title: Re: Lead time for Item / Weekends
entry.content:
I recommend to read the SAP explanation about how SAP uses the leadtime in the MRP

Example: Holidays, Lead Time, and Order Interval in MRP Calculations | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Settlement management for paying commissions
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have the following commissions process today.

  1. Sales rep participates as a partner on sales orders
  2. Commission is calculated using a condition type in pricing procedure
  3.  Upon invoicing the sale, commission is transferred to Vistex using an incentive document and accrued
  4. Every two weeks the accrued documents are settled using a settlement calendar and commission aggregated and sent to an external payroll system.

I believe settlement management should be able to handle the above business scenario. We are planning to move to S4 RISE PCE and looking to simplify our landscape.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: CALL TRANSFORMATION - CX_ST_MATCH_ELEMENT exception
entry.content: Nothing special. It's expecting what the message says. Probably an issue due to the data after what you show. Try with input XML containing only the lines you are showing. It should work. If so, analyze the other lines of the input XML.

entry.title: Re: FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG with Field names
entry.content: Look at the documentation. It says to use FIELD_TEXTS.

entry.title: Re: Deactivate tax codes
entry.content: But if you copy one PO which has a now inactive Tax Code to a new PO, the inactive Tax Code will be copied over without error.

entry.title: Re: Manager User Report - Export Content Name, versus UID
entry.content: Thank you!! I've sent to my client.

entry.title: Link the Trainer logo to return to library home page
entry.content: Hello, I have made the client's logo visible on the trainer bar and I read where it will link I thought to the beginning of the library but it's not.  The issue we have is if an end-user is sent a link to the simulation, there's no home button, or a way to navigate to the landing page menu of the library.  The only solutions I can think of is to create a hyperlink inside the simulation Description field to return to the Home Landing Page Menu of the library.  Or is there a way to add a Home button on the trainer bar?  Thank you!!

entry.title: Adding an non leading ledger in Live CFIN System (SAP Private cloud) for US GAAP
entry.content:
We need help in understanding the following business use case:

Our client currently is on CFIN system and its live hosted on Private cloud (Rise with SAP), Currently has three ledgers Leading ledger for IFRS, Local GAAP & Tax Ledger.

For Consolidation requirement, they want to add an additional ledger in CFIN, can you tell us the procedure for the same, need to following things:

1) Process to achieve that

2) Impact on current system

3) Extension ledger vs Additional Ledger difference, comparison and what should be the ideal way to achieve this

4) Impact on other modules like FI-AA etc and process to overcome those issues

entry.title: Re: How to obtain License Key to S/4 HANA On-Premise Syst...
entry.content:
Hello,

To generate licenses for On-Premise systems, this action must be performed by the customer. 
In the case of generating licenses for the SAP S/4 HANA On-Premise System, as outlined in KBA 2366280 - How to request a license key for SAP S/4HANA (all versions) - SAP for Me, it is necessary to access SAP for Me > License Keys and request the license by providing the required data, such as Product, System Version, System ID, System Name, System Type, Database, and Operating System. Additionally, you will need to select the license type and provide the hardware key.

Best regards,
SAP Support

This series kicks off this week with a discussion on data and AI strategy across SAP business applications, followed by a deep dive into our latest data offering, SAP Business Data Cloud. Additional topics include BWM modernization, SAP Databricks, and transition paths to SAP BBC. Building on this foundation, we invite you to validate your expertise by taking the new SAP Business Data Cloud certification exam. This roadshow will include three stops, and we will be focusing on the topic of getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core. The first stop will be on June 23rd in the very heart of New York City. I always love doing these short trips to New York City. And I know that my friend Antonio has some details on that. So over to you, Antonio.

This time, Rich and Tom will join the roadshow, and we will be doing CodeJams on ABAP Cloud and the Generative AI Hub. We kick things off with the Generative AI CodeJam in Sao Paulo, Brazil. And then we'll be doing both CodeJams in Lima, Peru, Mexico City, and Bogota, Colombia. Similar to last year, we will also do meetups in the different locations. entry.title: Re: Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio
entry.content:
Hi @Cocquerel,
Thanks for your questions.  No, Joule does not require MCP for interacting with custom Joule Skills. Given the nature of Skills, MCP is not necessary at this stage. However, for future Joule Agents, MCP will be leveraged when the use case calls for it.

MCP is still a relatively new protocol, and we are currently evaluating its applicability across different scenarios. We are actively exploring how a custom MCP server could be integrated as a tool within Joule Studio to support more advanced use cases.

entry.title: Re: Build, Deploy, and Extend AI Agents with Joule Studio
entry.content:
Hi @Waldemar_Koch,

Thanks for your queries. Please find my response below:

When will SAP Joule Studio be officially available?
Joule Studio with skill builder capability will be GA on 30th June.

How much will it cost?
We will publish commercial details soon once finalized. 

How flexible is SAP Joule Studio to connect non-SAP data sources? –
Joule Studio easily integrates with non-SAP data sources through API connectivity via actions in SAP Build.

When will there be reliable documentation for SAP Joule Studio?
Here is the link for the product webpage Joule Studio | AI Agent and Skill Builder. We will have more documentation once the product becomes GA.

entry.title: Is it possible to disable Business ByDesign Mobile App Notifications?
entry.content:
In the Mobile App, you are trying to remove activity notifications, but you are unable to do so.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to disable Business ByDesign Mobile Ap...
entry.content:
The Mobile App only allows to switch notifications 'On' or 'Off ' for the whole app, which can be done in the Notification settings of the device. However, the app won't allow you to switch off notification for a single module.

As soon as the App has been associated with SAP Business ByDesign and registered for notifications, you will get notifications on a regular base. This might even happen for modules that aren't scoped.

You can also refer to KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/3220887/

entry.title: Re: Settlement management for paying commissions
entry.content:
Hi @SekharCM, 

Yes, you should be able to manage that directly in SAP Settlement Management. 
If the sales rep is an external, you could copy one of the standard condition contract type 0CS1 to 0CS4. 
If the sales rep is an employee, you could copy one of the standard condition contract type 0CSA to 0CSD. 

Regarding the accrual, it can either be calculated and posted at the time of Billing document creation and release to accounting. 
Or you could post the accrual only with a Delta accrual at the required periodic time (every week, month, quarter, semester, custom). 
A periodic Settlement (partial or final) can be executed automatically. Up to you to post directly to accounting or only via proforma and set up a program to pick the amount of commissions to be paid to an external system. 


Regards, 
Gauthier

entry.title: Data Encryption in SAP Convergent Charging of PII data in CCIT before transfering to SAP CI/FICA?
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a possibility inside  SAP Convergent Charging where data encryption (using salt) can be done on a configurable set of master data objects like Business Partner, Contract Account and Subscription Contract within the rated consumptions before commiting the BIT to SAP Convergent Invoicing ? As consumption items can hold geolocation data on a traveller/consumer (Telco/Public transport sectors), once invoiced the BIT record does need encryption because it holds PII data and privacy/GDPR policies are to be respected for customers that did not provide consent for this data storage. Early archiving (even using SAP Information Lifecycle Management policies on BIT/CIT data nd deleting these record is no option as this data has a retention time of 3 months for our business purposes. Encryption would allow this data still to be used for marketing and financial revenue reporting purposes in SAP BI and other reporting tools.

entry.title: Re: Link the Trainer logo to return to library home page
entry.content:
Hi,

If you provide users with the URL of a Simulation, that's the only content the user will have access to when they open that URL.  If you want users to open a Simulation and have access to other content in a library, you must provide the URL of the library and specify which open should be displayed when the library opens.  For example, if the URL of my library is:

https://CompanyABC.enable-now.cloud.sap/wa/Adoption/index.html?show=group!GR_2D22D3E5B8124B7

and I want to open a Simulation within the library structure, I would amend the library URL to include the Simulation ID at the end of the URL.  So, I would provide users with a URL that looks like:

https://anzdemo1.enable-now.cloud.sap/wa/Adoption/index.html?show=group!GR_2D22D3E5B8124B7#project!PR_3B6A7B387811388C

With this additional detail on the end of the URL, the library will open and the specific Simulation included on the end of the URL will be highlighted within the librrary.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Lead Time Options
entry.content:
I’m working to develop a solution that separates the components of our Total Planned Delivery Lead Time into three distinct elements: Production Lead Time, Transportation Lead Time, and Other Lead Time Factors such as pre-shipment inspection.

Current State:
Our company outsources manufacturing to overseas suppliers under FOB Incoterms, meaning suppliers are responsible only for delivery to our freight forwarder at the origin port. From that point onward, we manage all transportation and final delivery to our distribution center. Currently, we maintain a single Total Planned Delivery Lead Time, which covers the full period from purchase order (PO) release to delivery at our distribution center.

Desired Future State:
We want to restructure the process as follows:

  • Production Lead Time: Maintained by the supplier and used for PO confirmation, based solely on the delivery to the origin port.
  • Transportation Lead Time: Managed and updated internally by our logistics team, without visibility to the supplier.
  • Inspection Lead Time: A variable component for cases requiring pre-shipment inspection by our supplier quality engineer.

Example Breakdown:
Total Planned Delivery Lead Time =

  • Production Lead Time: 90 Days
  • Transportation Lead Time: 60 Days
  • Source Inspection Lead Time: 7 Days
    = 157 Days Total

Do you have any system design ideas or process suggestions to support this future-state structure?

entry.title: Re: Not able to activate Trading partner Agreement with M...
entry.content:
Hi Venkatesh,

This is a limitation by SAP, reason being that when using custom message type system, the RD xsd which is used for message conversion will not be available in Integration Advisor, so that the agreement activation will fail with msgXSD is null exception.

However, Custom IDoc messages will work, provided they are with naming convention like MessageType.IDocType

Regards,

Shiveta

entry.title: “CPU Consumption Overview” Now Live for Private Cloud in SAP for Me Reporting
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the launch of the new CPU Consumption Overview in the Private Cloud Performance section of SAP for Me. This enhancement is a major step in our commitment to provide you with actionable transparency for your private cloud environments and enable smarter, data-driven decisions.

 The primary objective is to enable robust CPU capacity management through transparent monthly usage insights and early warnings about possible resource constraints.

By offering a comprehensive overview of CPU utilization across your systems and hosts, this reporting section brings transparency to ECS service performance and supports prompt, data-driven action when needed.

In the "CPU Overview" sub-section, you can quickly identify areas of critical CPU consumption, categorized by four severity levels: Green, Yellow, Orange, and Red. The severity level for each host is calculated as follows:

  1. CPU consumption of each host is continuously monitored and measured on an hourly basis.
  2. Measurements are classified into three categories:
    • Low Consumption: CPU usage below 30%
    • Medium Consumption: CPU usage between 30% and 70%
    • High Consumption: CPU usage above 70%

3. For this overview, we focus exclusively on periods of high CPU consumption, aggregating these measurements on a monthly basis. Each host receives a criticality rating based on the percentage of its high CPU measurements:

 

🟢 GREEN

≤ 10% HIGH CPU Measurements

🟡 YELLOW

> 10% and ≤ 30% HIGH CPU Measurements

🟠 ORANGE

> 30% and ≤ 50% HIGH CPU Measurements

🔴RED

> 50% HIGH CPU Measurements

The rating is calculated for each host, but the overview aggregates the data on the Software Product level.  The highest criticality level will be propagated from host to the system and from the system to the Software Product. If only one host (e.g from 20) has a critical rating for the month (e.g. red for  >50% High CPU measurements), the System will be rated red and the Software Product will be displayed as red.  This allows you to see already on the Software Product level when there is a critical CPU consumption for a host for this product. 

 While ratings are calculated per host, the overview presents data at the Software Product level. The highest criticality observed is propagated upwards: from host to system, and then from system to Software Product. For example, if just one out of twenty hosts is rated red for a given month, the entire system and the associated Software Product will also be marked as red. This approach enables you to quickly spot critical CPU consumption issues at a glance on the Software Product level, even if the problem stems from a single host.

To view more detailed information, simply expand the structure and navigate to the critical host. In the example below, the host with a red rating recorded 74% high CPU consumption measurements in April and 100% in May.

 You have access to several default filters to customize your view and focus on the data that matters most to you. Using the filters above the overview graph, you can, for example, restrict the display to only Production systems, adjust the reporting period to show data for 3, 6, or 12 months, or change the number of rows displayed in the overview.

Additionally, the filters in the top bar (highlighted in blue) allow you to further refine your results—enabling you to focus on a specific Solution Area, Software Product, or System. 

Please note that this CPU Consumption Overview is intended for data analysis, not for real-time or granular/hourly performance monitoring. It is a support tool for Capacity Planning and Infrastructure Optimization decisions.

In the "Consumption Details" sub-section, a stacked column graph displays, for each month, the number of hosts grouped by their criticality rating. Directly below the graph, a table provides detailed breakdowns by host and by month.

When you select a segment in the graph (for example, a red portion), both the graph and table will update to reflect your selection. Typically, critical CPU consumption is rare and most hosts appear as green in the graph. To focus more effectively on hosts with critical consumption, you have several options:

  • Sort the table by Rating: This brings hosts with the highest proportion of HIGH CPU measurements to the top.
  • Select a segment in the graph: Click on a specific colored segment (such as red) to filter both the graph and table accordingly.
  • Customize the graph: Exclude non-critical hosts to display only those with HIGH CPU consumption measurements (removing hosts rated green, with less than 10% high CPU consumption).

In the details subsection, you can also filter by System Role (e.g., show only productive systems) and by the desired time period.

This  new reporting provides a quick and intuitive overview of CPU consumption across your landscape, helping you identify and address critical hosts efficiently.

Find the new CPU Consumption Overview feature under Private Cloud Performance in SAP for Me Reporting. Use the available filters to tailor your view and quickly drill down to hosts with critical statuses for immediate action.

SAP for Me is made for you!

Stay updated by subscribing to this blog or visiting the SAP Support Portal / Customer Insights.

Related blogs: all related blogs to ‘Customer Insights’ reporting.

entry.title: Re: Manage My Team Tile Error: "An application error occurred"
entry.content:
This issue can have various underlying causes and it’s difficult to pinpoint without directly reviewing the system setup.

However, based on similar scenarios, I suggest to check my suggestions listed below that I believe can help point you in the right direction:

 

  • Ask the manager to initiate the same action manually: Have them go to a direct report’s People Profile, then select Take Action / All Actions to try and action the change. If the error appears here too, it’s likely related to permissions or data configuration rather than the tile itself.

 

  • Proxy as the manager: If you have admin access, use the Proxy Now feature to replicate the issue under the manager’s account. This helps confirm whether the issue is user-specific or systemic.

 

  • Use the RBP Troubleshooting Tool: Compare the affected manager’s permissions with another manager who is able to use the Manage My Team tile. Check for any discrepancies, especially under ‘Job Information’, ‘Employee Data’, ‘Employee Central Effective Dated Entities’.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and Central Finance
entry.content:
Hi Lena,

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, using Central Finance (CFIN) as a System-of-Report is limited. Full journal entry replication is not supported. CFIN is not part of the scope of the Public Cloud. Only specific data (like supplier invoices) might be exposed via APIs or integration, but standard CFIN replication via SLT is unavailable.

Central Credit Management is available as a standalone function in Public Cloud, but cannot be used in a CFIN scenario since CFIN is not supported in the public edition.

Group Reporting in Public Cloud is the recommended approach for reporting and consolidation, with integration from multiple S/4HANA Public Cloud tenants via APIs or staging layers, not CFIN.

Thank you and. Best regards,

Chuma

entry.title: SAP ChaRM TOC
entry.content:
Our current ChaRM is using a 4-system landscape where during E004 TOCs are getting imported to S4D 200---> S4D 100, S4D 400, S4Q 100 and S4Q 101 to both S4D and S4Q systems, now we want to restrict TOC movement only to S4D 100 and S4D 400 and not to S4Q 100 and S4Q 101 and only the actual TR should move to S4Q clients and not the TOCs. How can be achieve that?

S4D 200 ( Development client ) ---> S4D 100, S4D 400, S4Q 100 and S4Q 101 ( All QA clients ) ---> S2T 100 ( Pre production client)  --- S4P 100 ( Production client 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP ChaRM TOC
entry.content:
Hi,

You need to split your transport target group for S4D/100, S4D/400,S4Q/100 and S4Q/101 into 2 target groups, first group includes S4D/100 S4D/400, second group S4Q/100 and S4Q/101. And you configure the first group as the first target system of S4D/200. 

There is no standard way to import ToC into some systems only but not for other systems in the same transport target group.

BR

entry.content:
Hello Osman,

The scope "uaa.reource" doesn't mean full access to Cloud ALM, it is part of all client credential tokens for ensuring that user authentication is enforced when accessing the SAP Cloud ALM. 

You may refer to the KB blow to find out the scopes assigned to the service key.
##3481257 - How to find out the scopes already assigned to an SAP Cloud ALM API Service Instance? 

best regards,
Peng

entry.title: Re: SAC vs Tableau vs Power BI
entry.content:
Choosing SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC): What Sets It Apart from Other Leading Analytics Tools

This is a very valid and increasingly common concern when evaluating SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) against leading analytics tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Qlik. While many of these platforms offer similar capabilities—such as visualizations, real-time connectivity, and AI integration—SAC provides unique advantages, especially in SAP-centric environments.

Below is a breakdown of what truly differentiates SAC, from both technical and strategic perspectives:

Key Advantages of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

1. Native, Deep Integration with the SAP Ecosystem
Tight Coupling with SAP Systems
SAC is built with native integration for:

SAP S/4HANA

SAP BW/4HANA

SAP Datasphere

SAP BPC (for hybrid planning scenarios)

Live Data Connectivity with Zero Data Replication
SAC connects to SAP sources live, without replicating or duplicating data. This ensures:

Real-time insights

Strong data governance

No need for additional ETL or data layers

Semantic Layer Understanding
SAC consumes SAP-native artifacts such as:

CDS Views from S/4HANA

BW Queries

Datasphere Models
preserving business logic and hierarchies without extra effort.

Real-Time Transactional Drilldowns
Users can:

Drill down from KPIs (e.g., Revenue)

Reach line items or source documents

Do this all live without extra integrations

2. Built-In Planning Capabilities (A Major Differentiator)

SAC uniquely combines BI + Planning + Predictive in one platform.

Advanced Planning Functions:

Versioning (Actuals, Budgets, Forecasts)

Currency conversion

Data entry, spreading, and driver-based planning

Allocations & multi-step data actions

Task & Workflow Management:

Calendar-based task planning

Workflow assignments for team collaboration

Major Benefit for FP&A Teams:
Replaces multiple tools (Excel, BPC, etc.) with a unified environment for the full planning cycle—from budgeting to reporting.

3. Unified Platform with Lower Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)

Comprehensive Capabilities:

Business Intelligence (Storytelling, Dashboards)

Augmented & Predictive Analytics

Integrated Planning

Simplified Architecture:

No need for multiple tools or vendors

Lower licensing, integration, and maintenance overhead

Built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP):

Ensures enterprise scalability and innovation

4. Security & Authorization Consistency

Reuses Existing SAP Security Models:

Inherits roles from BW, S/4HANA, and Datasphere

No need to recreate authorization logic in external tools

Built-in Row-Level Security:

Automatically applies SAP-level user data restrictions

Ideal for Regulated Industries:

Ensures auditability and compliance (e.g., pharma, finance)

5. Embedded AI and Predictive Analytics

Smart Features:

Smart Insights, Smart Discovery, Time Series Forecasting

Explainable AI (e.g., Smart Predict, Smart Grouping)

Contextual Machine Learning:

Better predictions using SAP master and transactional data

No-Code Experience:

Business users can apply ML without needing coding or data science skills

6. Strategic Roadmap Alignment with SAP

SAP’s Long-Term Strategy:

Focus is on SAC + Datasphere for future analytics and planning

Older tools like BPC, BusinessObjects, and Lumira are in maintenance

Future-Proof Investment:

Continuous updates and innovations

Avoid future migration costs from legacy tools


Final Thoughts: When to Choose SAC

If your organization is SAP-centric and you want to:

Leverage live, real-time data directly from SAP

Reuse existing SAP investments and security

Perform planning and forecasting within the same environment

Avoid redundant data movement and integration efforts

Align with SAP’s innovation roadmap

Then SAP Analytics Cloud is not just an option—it’s the most strategic choice.

entry.title: 入門！SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning 機能紹介シリーズ - 予測
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのPlanning機能をこれから学ぶ方を対象とし、予算計画などの一連の予算管理業務に必要なSAP Analytics Cloudのそれぞれの機能の特徴や要点をご紹介します。

※機能の詳細ついてはSAP Help Portalを参照ください。

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2025年6月に作成しています。）

本ブログでは、SAP Analytics Cloudにおける予測についてご紹介します。

目次：

  • SAP Analytics Cloudの予測計画機能 概要
  • 予測シナリオ（Smart Predict） 概要
  • 予測シナリオ（Smart Predict）設定方法
  • （参考）ストーリーでの時系列予測機能

SAP Analytics Cloudの予測計画機能 概要

SAP Analytics Cloudでは統計処理による各種の予測計画の機能が用意されています。機能の利用にあたっては専門的な統計知識などを必要とせず、容易に利用することが可能です。

SAP Analytics Cloudで実行可能な予測計画の方法とそれぞれの特徴は以下です。

①予測シナリオ（Smart Predict）：各種パラメータ設定に基づく高度な予測モデル作成

  • コーディングなしで設定可能で、機械学習やプログラミングの知識がなくてもパラメータ設定により予測を行えます。
  • 実行可能な予測シナリオは以下3つです。
    • 分類　（例：従業員属性に基づく離職予測など）
    • 回帰　（例：需要予測モデルの作成など）
    • 時系列予測　（例：過去の販売データに基づく将来予測値の作成など）

②ストーリーでの時系列予測機能：容易な操作での予測値作成

  • 時系列/折れ線グラフの時系列予測
  • テーブルの時系列予測

本ブログでは①の予測シナリオ（Smart Predict）についてご紹介します。

予測シナリオ（Smart Predict） 概要

予測シナリオ（Smart Predict）では、機械学習やプログラミングの知識がなくても簡単に予測を行うことができます。

一般的な予測モデル作成プロセスとして、「課題の設定」「データ準備」「予測モデル作成」「結果の確認」「予測モデルの適用」「結果の利用」があり、予測シナリオ（Smart Predict）は「予測モデル結果の確認」「予測モデルの適用」「結果の利用」を自動化します。

 

予測シナリオ（Smart Predict）では、あらかじめ用意された分類、回帰、時系列の三つのシナリオを使用することが可能です。

  • 分類：正解となる二分値と入力変数の組み合わせを学習し、未知のデータ分類を予想（利用例：過去キャンペーンの応答実績からキャンペーンに反応しやすい顧客属性を予測）
  • 回帰：数値を相関のある変数の変動にもとづいて予測し、個々の数値の背後にある主要な要因を探索（利用例：売れ筋商品の要因の分析）
  • 時系列：継続するヒストリカルデータからトレンド、周期性および季節性を特定し、データの推移を予測（利用例：過去データに基づく予算の初期値を作成）

本ブログでは計画での利用が多いと考えられる時系列のシナリオについて紹介します。
計画モデルにデータ更新をするのは、時系列のみです。

予測シナリオ（Smart Predict）設定方法

時系列のシナリオで過去データに基づく飲料の売上予測を作成する例を用いて紹介します。

①予測シナリオを新規で作成します。今回は「時系列予測」を選択します。

②データソースとして対象のモデルまたはデータセットを選択します。※時系列シナリオのみデータソースとして計画モデルを選択、また予測結果を計画モデルに統合することが可能です。

③予測パラメータの設定を行います。

ターゲット（予測対象）や予測期間、エンティティ（予測対象）などのパラメータ設定を行います。
各設定項目の詳細は SAP Help Portal 計画向けに時系列予測モデルの設定を定義 および 計画モデルにインフルエンサを追加すると、予測モデルの精度がどのように向上する可能性があるか? をご確認ください。

④予測を実行します。

⑤結果を確認します。予測モデル構造や予測モデルのパフォーマンスについてはビジュアルなレポートで確認することができます。

予測モデルの結果のレポートの詳細については SAP Help Portal 時系列予測モデルの結果の分析 をご確認ください。

⑥作成した予測を元の計画モデルに保存します。予測は非公開バージョンに対して保存できます。

予測を保存すると、チャートやテーブル上でも作成した予測値を利用することができます。作成した予測値をもとに予算・予測比較を行うことや予算計画の初期値として利用することが可能です。

（参考）ストーリーでの時系列予測機能

ストーリー上のチャートやテーブルに対して数クリックの容易な操作で予測を作成することができます。
時系列チャートまたは折れ線チャートで時系列予測を実行できます。詳細についてはSAP Help Portal 時系列チャートまたは折れ線チャートでの予測の実行（最適化済み） をご確認ください。

※2025年6月現在、最適化されたストーリーエクスペリエンスでは、テーブルの時系列予測がサポートされません。

終わりに

今回は予測のご紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは予算計画などの一連の予算管理業務に必要な機能を備えており、本ブログシリーズではそれぞれの機能の特徴や要点をご紹介しています。

こちらのリンクからご参照ください。

entry.title: Re: Execute "BAPI_BUPA_PFCT_GETFUNCTDETAIL" in RPA
entry.content:
Hi Mateusz,

I checked that Bapi:

  REFRESH:

    et_data,

    et_return.

    IF cl_crm_rfc=>check_rfc_external_direct( ) = abap_true.

*   Function not supported

    MESSAGE x006(sr).

  ENDIF.

This BAPI should be restricted, you can't call it from external system.

It's also mentioned in note: 2670866

Regards,

Jun Chu

entry.title: How to restrict access to certain fields of upload in Mass upload apps in Fiori
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are moving towards the clean core post s/4 HANA migration and trying to make the processes standard as possible and avoid custom code changes.

There are multiple custom programs which allow users to mass update certain fields in material master according to their roles and departments. So, we have Fiori apps that cater to mass updates but giving them access to these Fiori apps will allow them to update all the fields. So how do we restrict to allow them to upload only  certain fields or do we continue to edit and cater these needs through our custom programs?

Please suggest for a best approach so we can avoid the custom code changes.

 

Thanks

Gowthami

entry.title: Outbound Delivery Date
entry.content:
Hello 

I have a query on the date that is being held in the outbound delivery. 

For example: SO 26082/20 created on 27/05/2025. This line (20) confirmed by PO (4627) for delivery on 01/07/2025. On the 04/06/2025 the outbound delivery for this line was created as stock available from another PO received early (from PO 3850).  The outbound created still has a date of 01/07 from the original PO. 

My questions:

- As there has been sudden receipts (in this example from PO 3850) why wasn’t this date used in the outbound?

- As the outbound will only be created when there is stock available (which happened) so shouldn’t the outbound date be updated for the day available (04/06)? Why doesn’t the outbound date update to reflect it (04/06)?

The business need is if stock comes in early and it is available to be used the outbounds should be created with the date stock received (not holding onto a date in the future). This ensures goods are delivered when material available. 

Adam

entry.title: Request Support for Custom logback.xml Deployment on SAP BTP (tomee7)
entry.content:
I am currently deploying a Java application to the SAP BTP environment, using the following setup:

  • TARGET_RUNTIME: tomee7
  • Deployment Type: Java WAR application

My application includes a custom logback.xml configuration for logging purposes, which is placed under src/main/resources. However, upon deployment, I noticed that the application seems to use the default logback.xml provided by the tomee7 runtime, rather than the one included in my application package.

I would appreciate your support in clarifying the following:

  1. Is it possible to override the default logback.xml used by the tomee7 runtime with a custom configuration bundled in my WAR file?
  2. If yes, could you please advise:
    • The correct placement of the logback.xml file within the WAR structure?
    • Any additional configuration required to ensure the runtime uses my custom file?
  3. If not, is there a supported mechanism to achieve custom logging configurations within the tomee7 runtime on BTP?

entry.title: UPS API integration via SAP ABAP
entry.content:
Hello Everyone, 

I am trying to find ways to integrate UPS API's via SAP ABAP code .  

Have created a shipper account in the UPS developer portal and trying to create an APP on the developer portal , however I am unable to picture what callback URL to provide in there .  

There is lot of information out there to get these API's integrated via an external website and I think I understand the general concept like below 

1. Using the client id and client secret the external website will first get the Access code 

2. Access code is returned to the call back URL that is specified at the time of creating the app 

3. Using the access code another request is made from the sender to get the access token 

4. Once the access token is received any UPS API could be called with it and no additional authentication would be required

Any steps/info to implement UPS API integration directly from ABAP code would be very helpful 

Is it possible to do it just using ABAP system ? or a middleware like SAP CPI would be mandatory to be included here .  

Thanks,

Santosh.

entry.title: HU Label not printed for HUs loaded as initial stock balances via Migration Cockpit

As part of the cutover, we need to print the HU label and stick it on every pallet (approximately 10,000). This is a showstopper for go-live. 

I tried printing from Warehouse Monitor but have click send record for every HU, means have to click 10,000 times

entry.title: Re: Treating Business Goals as "Individual" Goals possible?
entry.content:
@mkrieg 

If you want to show these values into a Goal section you don't have a choice I'm afraid but to use either the Team or Indiv section and use the dummy PM form logic you describe (and if the values can be different by record then use ALCF configured and different PM forms)

If you are fine with showing these values elsewhere (= not in a goal section) then you could consider storing them in an MDF object to retrieve them into employee history and then customize your calculations accordingly.

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: S_ALR_87013019-How to add another language key for the report layout
Provisioning of Business Data Cloud : SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
As a continuation of the series of the Introductory Guide to Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud and its various components, this blog will help you in provisioning SAP Datasphere within Business Data Cloud. Please refer to the different parts of the blog to get a holistic understanding of the entire process.

Part 1: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud through SAP4Me
Part 2: Provisioning of SAP Datasphere
Part 3: Provisioning of SAP Analytics Cloud
Part 4: Provisioning of SAP Databricks
Part 5: Installation of Intelligent Apps and Data Packages

#Part 3

In this blog, we will discuss in detail how to provision SAP Analytics Cloud via Business Data Cloud Core.

  1. Begin the provisioning of BDC apps individually, for example, you can start with SAP Analytics Cloud

    Image 1
  2. In the "Provision SAP Analytics Cloud" dialog that appears, provide details such as Solution Name, Resource Group, and Business Type

    Image 2
  3. In the Business Type, you can choosw between 'Test' or 'Production'
  4. In the Quota Assignment section, specify the number of users for the tenant. This number impacts the capacity units assigned to SAP Analytics Cloud from the total capacity units available for the SAP Business Data Cloud solution.
  5. In case of a Test Environment, you must provide a minimum of 20 users

    Image 3
  6. In case you choose a Production Environment, you must provide either a minimum of 25 BI users OR a minimum of 1 Planning Professional + 10 Planning Standard users. Any combination of the available license types is allowed, as long as this minimum requirement is fulfilled

    Image 4
  7. The configure parameters section includes input details such as region, the system owner's email ID, and name.  Select the same region previously chosen for provisioning the BDC Cockpit

    Image 5
  8. As a next step, review them, and then submit the information
  9. A confirmation message informs you that the provisioning request has been sent
  10. Click "View in Resources" to see the tenant within the indicated resource group.
  11. In the Resources tab, the status of the solution is "Processing" and will change to "Ready" once the provisioning is complete
  12. The system owner of SAP Analytics Cloud, specified during provisioning, receives an email notification when the tenant is provisioned
  13. Once the tenant is ready, you will be able to access the tenant from the link under the ‘Resource’ tab and under the tenant details
  14. Under ‘Customer Landscape’, you can see the system details of the new tenant. Also, the system integration is visually presented, making it easier to understand and navigate through your system landscape
  15. As a next step, you will have to add the tenant to the ‘Formation’, that you have created already and added your BDC tenant to it
  16. Navigate to me.sap.com > Products & portfolio > Business Data Cloud > Customer Landscape > Formation
  17. Choose the correct Formation and then click on ‘Include System’ and choose the SAC tenant to include in the formation, and save the changes

    Image 6

     Image 7

  18. A single SAP Analytics Cloud tenant can only be part of one formation and not multiple formations

    Now, you have successfully provisioned a SAP Analytics Cloud tenant within the BDC Core.

Keep an eye on the entire series for the Blog Post.
Happy Learning

Regards,
Shreyasri Bose

entry.title: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud : SAP Databricks
entry.content:
As a continuation of the series of the Introductory Guide to Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud and its various components, this blog will help you in provisioning SAP Datasphere within Business Data Cloud. Please refer to the different parts to get a holistic understanding of the entire process.

Part 1: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud through SAP4Me
Part 2: Provisioning of SAP Datasphere
Part 3: Provisioning of SAP Analytics Cloud
Part 4: Provisioning of SAP Databricks
Part 5: Installation of Intelligent Apps and Data Packages

#Part 4

In this blog, we will discuss in detail how to provision SAP Databricks via Business Data Cloud Core.

  1. With the completion of the provisioning of SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud,  you can start with SAP Databricks
  2. In the "Provision SAP Databricks" dialog that appears, provide details such as Solution Name, Resource Group, and Business Type.  You can select the business type from ‘Test’ or 'Production’
  3. The Quota  Amount is not enforced. You can use SAP Databricks even if you do not indicate capacity units for it. Billing is always according to SAP Databricks' actual usage and not according to the indicated quota. You can just select '1' and move to the next step
  4. Select the Cloud Identity Service tenant that is configured with your BTP cockpit
  5. Select the same region previously chosen for provisioning the BDC Cockpit
  6. Input details in the configure parameters section, such as the owner’s email, name, etc.
  7. In SAP Databricks, your SAP Databricks tenant is implemented in the form of an account. Provide the account name. You can use the resource name. In case you have several SAP Databricks accounts, use names that will allow you to distinguish them
  8. Select the Formation where you want to add the Databricks tenant
    Provide the name for the formation that contains the SAP Databricks tenant and the IAS system you selected in a previous tab. In a separate step, you will create a formation of 'Business Data Cloud' type containing SAP Databricks and the other SAP Business Data Cloud systems, see Creating SAP Business Data Cloud Formations.
  9. A confirmation message informs you that the provisioning request has been sent. Click "View in Resources" to see the tenant within the indicated resource group
  10. In the Resources tab, the status of the solution is "Processing" and will change to "Ready" once the provisioning is complete
  11. The system owner of SAP Databricks, specified during provisioning, receives an email notification when the tenant is provisioned
  12. Once the tenant is ready, you will be able to access the tenant from the link under the ‘Resource’ tab and under the tenant details
  13. Under ‘Customer Landscape’, you can see the system details of the new tenant. Also, the system integration is visually presented, making it easier to understand and navigate through your system landscape
  14. As a next step, you will have to add the tenant to the ‘Formation’, that you have created already and added your BDC tenant to it
  15. Navigate to me.sap.com > Products & portfolio > Business Data Cloud > Customer Landscape > Formation
  16. Choose the correct Formation and then click on ‘Include System’ and choose the Databricks tenant to include in the formation, and save the changes
  17. A single Databricks tenant can only be part of one formation and not multiple formations

entry.title: Re: Sales order availability check
entry.content:
Hi, Need immediate help as its happening in our production system.

We tried to replicate the same by passing the same I-docs in Quality system but all worked fine in Quality.

The issue started occuring mostly either due to below 2 reasons.

1. We upgraded to S4 2023 version 

2. We made a change in schedule line category determination of Parent material . It was not a shipable and only components were getting shipped. So we update the assignment in VOV5 for item category of parent material to mark it not relevant for delivery (CD schedule line category was assigned).  Unnecessary entries in MD04 were getting created for Parent Material as we ship only the components. Item category for parent and BOM is different. BOM has a relevant for Delivery Schedule line category.

Previously Same was assigned to Parent material also , resulting in entries in MD04 and sales order remaining open. 

3. The issue is occuring randamly and is happening for sales orders without any BUndle setup also.( no BOM exists)

entry.title: EDI - Sales Orders
entry.content:
Hello 

How do others do EDI for SO? 

Our current setup is orders from customers go to middle ware company and then get posted into SAP. 

If there are any issues with SO (material # incorrect etc) the SO wont be posed into SAP and remains in the middle ware company until date matches. 

Is there a way that SO, even with incorrect data, can go into SAP and be held for review before being created as a SO? 

Wondering how others deal with this issue?

Adam
